meta_title: For Freelancers: Git with AI to Solve no design skills (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no design skills. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Git with AI: Solve No Design Skills for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with UI design. Learn to use Git, Antigravity, and Claude Code to build professional-grade interfaces as a solo freelancer in minutes.
content.answer_block: You don't need a design degree; you need an AI-augmented development stack. By integrating Antigravity for rapid UI scaffolding and Claude Code for intelligent Git versioning, you can translate technical requirements into pixel-perfect interfaces. This workflow automates design decisions, ensuring professional results while you focus on shipping client features faster.
content.intro:
The blank canvas is a freelancer's greatest enemy. You have the backend logic and the database schema, but when it comes to the UI, you freeze. Clients demand polished, modern interfaces, and your lack of design expertise is becoming a bottleneck that threatens your project timelines and your bottom line.

But the era of the 'designer-developer' divide is over. With the right AI-driven Git workflow, you can bypass the design phase entirely. By leveraging Antigravity to generate high-fidelity UI components and Claude Code to manage your repository, you turn your technical specifications into production-ready code, allowing you to ship high-value work at record speeds.

steps.title: Initialize Your Repository with Claude
steps.description: Start by setting up a clean Git environment that allows Claude Code to track your architectural changes in real-time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a professional SaaS boilerplate with Tailwind CSS and Shadcn UI components.

steps.title: Define UI Requirements via Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the visual layout based on your functional requirements, ensuring accessibility and modern aesthetics.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive dashboard layout for a SaaS project including a sidebar, top navigation, and a data-driven stats grid using Tailwind.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply the generated layout to src/components/layout.tsx'

steps.title: Iterative Refinement with Claude Code
steps.description: Use the CLI to refine specific components without manually tweaking CSS classes, maintaining design consistency.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Adjust the color palette to a professional indigo-slate theme and ensure mobile responsiveness.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'update color theme to indigo-slate and fix mobile nav overlap'

steps.title: Automated Semantic Commits
steps.description: Ensure your project history is clean and professional by letting AI write your commit messages based on changes made.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current codebase and generate a summary for a git commit.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit --message 'feat: implement responsive dashboard layout'

steps.title: Deployment and Final Polish
steps.description: Finalize your build and push to your git remote, ensuring your project is ready for client review.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Verify that all components meet WCAG accessibility standards and suggest necessary aria-labels.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add aria-labels to all interactive components' && git push origin main

pro_tips: Always instruct your AI to use a design system like Shadcn; it prevents 'creative' CSS and keeps your UI looking like a premium SaaS product.
Use 'claude diff' before every commit to catch AI hallucinations that might have injected unnecessary bloat into your CSS.
Keep your Antigravity prompts focused on structure and spacing, letting the CSS framework handle the visual polish.
Create a 'design-system.json' file in your root directory; point Claude to this file to maintain consistent padding and font sizes across all modules.
Use Git branches for every new UI feature—this allows you to 'undo' a bad design iteration in seconds without breaking your main build.
content.next_steps: Once your first dashboard is live, start building a library of reusable UI components in your Git repository to make your future client projects even faster.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting design anxiety stall your freelance business. With this Git-AI stack, you can deliver agency-level work without the agency-level overhead. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
